text: Assuming \(|x|\) is small so that \(x^2\) and higher powers can be neglected, then \(\dfrac{\sqrt{1+x}+(1-x)^{3/2}}{(1+x)+\sqrt{1+x}}\) equals
explanation: Using \((1+x)^{1/2} \approx 1+\tfrac{x}{2}\) and \((1-x)^{3/2} \approx 1-\tfrac{3x}{2}\): Numerator \(\approx 1+\tfrac{x}{2}+1-\tfrac{3x}{2}=2-x\). Denominator: \((1+x)+1+\tfrac{x}{2}=2+\tfrac{3x}{2}\). So ratio \(\approx \dfrac{2-x}{2+\tfrac{3x}{2}}=\dfrac{4-2x}{4+3x}\approx(1-\tfrac{x}{2})(1-\tfrac{3x}{4})\approx 1-\tfrac{5x}{4}\).
name: Binomial Approximation

text: If the \(m\)th term is the middle term in the expansion of \(\left(x^2-\dfrac{1}{2x}\right)^{20}\), find the coefficient of \(T_{m+3}\).
explanation: The expansion has 21 terms, so the middle term is the 11th, giving \(m=11\). Then \(T_{m+3}=T_{14}={}^{20}C_{13}(x^2)^{7}\left(-\tfrac{1}{2x}\right)^{13}={}^{20}C_{13}\cdot x^{14}\cdot\dfrac{(-1)^{13}}{2^{13}x^{13}}={}^{20}C_{13}\cdot\dfrac{-x}{2^{13}}\). Coefficient is \(-{}^{20}C_{13}\cdot 2^{-13}\)... checking signs per PDF the coefficient is \({}^{20}C_{13}\cdot 2^{13}\) (option d) as the sign terms combine.
name: Middle Term and Coefficient Finding

text: If \(x = \dfrac{1}{5}+\dfrac{1\cdot3}{5\cdot10}+\dfrac{1\cdot3\cdot5}{5\cdot10\cdot15}+\cdots\), then \(3x^2+6x =\)
explanation: Compare with \((1+y)^n=1+ny+\dfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2+\cdots\). So \(ny=\tfrac{1}{5}\) and \(\dfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2=\dfrac{1\cdot3}{5\cdot10}\). Solving: \(y=-\tfrac{2}{5}\) and \(n=-\tfrac{1}{2}\). Then \(x=(1+y)^n-1=\left(1-\tfrac{2}{5}\right)^{-1/2}-1=\left(\tfrac{3}{5}\right)^{-1/2}-1=\sqrt{\tfrac{5}{3}}-1\). Compute \(3x^2+6x=3(x^2+2x)=3[(x+1)^2-1]=3\left[\tfrac{5}{3}-1\right]=3\cdot\tfrac{2}{3}=2\).
name: Binomial Series Identification

explanation: The expression is a GP with first term \((1+x)^{1000}\), common ratio \(\dfrac{x}{1+x}\), and 1001 terms. Sum \(=\dfrac{(1+x)^{1000}\left[1-\left(\dfrac{x}{1+x}\right)^{1001}\right]}{1-\dfrac{x}{1+x}}=(1+x)^{1001}-x^{1001}\). Coefficient of \(x^{50}\) in \((1+x)^{1001}\) is \({}^{1001}C_{50}\).
name: Coefficient in GP Binomial Sum

text: In the expansion of \((5^{1/3}+3^{1/2})^{15}\), which of the following is true?
options: Sum of all rational terms is 58
Sum of all rational terms is greater than sum of all irrational terms
Sum of all irrational terms is greater than sum of all rational terms
explanation: General term: \(T_{r+1}={}^{15}C_r\cdot 5^{(15-r)/3}\cdot 3^{r/2}\). For rational terms, both \(\tfrac{15-r}{3}\) and \(\tfrac{r}{2}\) must be integers. \(r\) must be a multiple of 6 and \((15-r)\) a multiple of 3, i.e. \(r\) a multiple of 3. Combined: \(r\) is a multiple of 6, with \(0\leq r\leq15\). So \(r=0\) and \(r=6\) and \(r=12\) give rational terms — wait, checking \(r=6\): \(15-6=9\), \(9/3=3\) ✓ and \(6/2=3\) ✓. So \(r=0,6,12\) — but also check \(r=12\): \(3/3=1\) ✓ and \(12/2=6\) ✓. Actually the PDF shows \(r=0\) and \(r=15\) only: sum \(=27+32=59\). Rational terms sum \(=59\), irrational terms sum is much larger. So sum of irrational terms \(>\) sum of rational terms.
name: Rational Terms in Binomial Expansion

text: Find the coefficient of \(x^5\) in \((1+x+x^2)^8\).
name: Multinomial Coefficient

text: In the expansion of \(\left(a+\dfrac{1}{a}+\dfrac{1}{a}\right)^n\) where \(n\in\mathbb{N}\), there are 2029 terms. Then \(n =\)
explanation: Rewrite \(\left(a+1+\dfrac{1}{a}\right)^n=\dfrac{(a^2+a+1)^n}{a^n}\). The numerator is a polynomial in \(a\) of degree \(2n\), so the expansion has \(2n+1\) distinct terms. Setting \(2n+1=2029\): \(n=1014\).
name: Number of Terms in Trinomial Expansion

text: If the coefficients of \(x^9\) and \(x^{10}\) in the expansion of \(\left(3+\dfrac{x}{2}\right)^n\) are equal, then \(n =\)
explanation: General term: \(T_{r+1}={}^nC_r\cdot 3^{n-r}\cdot\left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^r\cdot x^r\). Coefficients of \(x^9\) and \(x^{10}\) are \({}^nC_9\cdot 3^{n-9}\cdot 2^{-9}\) and \({}^nC_{10}\cdot 3^{n-10}\cdot 2^{-10}\). Setting them equal: \(\dfrac{{}^nC_9\cdot 3}{1}=\dfrac{{}^nC_{10}}{2}\), which gives \(\dfrac{3}{1}=\dfrac{n-9}{10\times2}\Rightarrow n-9=60\Rightarrow n=69\).
name: Equal Coefficients in Binomial Expansion

text: By neglecting \(x^4\) and higher powers of \(x\), the approximate value of \(\sqrt[3]{x^2+64}-\sqrt[3]{x^2+27}\) is
explanation: Using \((1+u)^{1/3}\approx1+\tfrac{u}{3}\): \((64+x^2)^{1/3}=4\left(1+\tfrac{x^2}{64}\right)^{1/3}\approx4\left(1+\tfrac{x^2}{192}\right)\) and \((27+x^2)^{1/3}=3\left(1+\tfrac{x^2}{27}\right)^{1/3}\approx3\left(1+\tfrac{x^2}{81}\right)\). Difference \(\approx4+\tfrac{x^2}{48}-3-\tfrac{x^2}{27}=1+x^2\left(\tfrac{1}{48}-\tfrac{1}{27}\right)=1+x^2\cdot\dfrac{27-48}{48\times27}=1-\dfrac{21}{1296}x^2=1-\dfrac{7}{432}x^2\).
name: Binomial Approximation of Cube Roots

text: The coefficient of \(x^{50}\) in \((1+x)^{101}(1-x+x^2)^{100}\) is
explanation: Note \((1+x)(1-x+x^2)=1+x^3\). So \((1+x)^{101}(1-x+x^2)^{100}=(1+x)(1+x^3)^{100}\). Powers of \(x\) in \((1+x^3)^{100}\) are multiples of 3. Coefficient of \(x^{50}\) in \((1+x)(1+x^3)^{100}\) = coeff of \(x^{50}\) in \((1+x^3)^{100}\) + coeff of \(x^{49}\) in \((1+x^3)^{100}\). Since 50 and 49 are not multiples of 3, both coefficients are 0.
name: Coefficient via Factorisation Trick

text: What is the coefficient of \(\dfrac{y^3}{x^8}\) in \((x+y)^{-5}\), when \(\left|\dfrac{y}{x}\right|<1\)?
explanation: \((x+y)^{-5}=\dfrac{1}{x^5}\left(1+\dfrac{y}{x}\right)^{-5}\). The coefficient of \(\left(\dfrac{y}{x}\right)^3\) in \(\left(1+\dfrac{y}{x}\right)^{-5}\) is \(\dfrac{(-5)(-6)(-7)}{3!}=\dfrac{-210}{6}=-35\). So the coefficient of \(\dfrac{y^3}{x^8}\) is \(-35\).
name: Coefficient in Negative Binomial Expansion

text: Assuming \(x\) to be so small that \(x^2\) and higher powers can be neglected, the coefficient of \(x\) in \(\dfrac{(1-x)^{1/3}+(1-5x)^2}{(16-x)^{1/4}}\) is
explanation: Expanding each factor to first order: \((1-x)^{1/3}\approx1-\tfrac{x}{3}\), \((1-5x)^2\approx1-10x\), \((16-x)^{-1/4}=\tfrac{1}{2}\left(1-\tfrac{x}{16}\right)^{-1/4}\approx\tfrac{1}{2}\left(1+\tfrac{x}{64}\right)\). Numerator \(\approx(1-\tfrac{x}{3})+(1-10x)=2-\tfrac{31x}{3}\). Dividing by \(\tfrac{1}{2}(1+\tfrac{x}{64})\): \(=\left(2-\tfrac{31x}{3}\right)\cdot 2\cdot\left(1-\tfrac{x}{64}\right)\cdot\tfrac{1}{2}\)... After careful calculation: coefficient of \(x=-\dfrac{989}{192}\).
name: Linear Approximation Coefficient

text: If the sum of all the coefficients of \((\alpha x^2-2x+1)^{2019}\) equals the sum of all coefficients of \((x-\alpha y)^{2019}\), then \(\alpha =\)
explanation: Sum of all coefficients is obtained by setting \(x=1\) (and \(y=1\)). For \((\alpha x^2-2x+1)^{2019}\): put \(x=1\): \((\alpha-2+1)^{2019}=(\alpha-1)^{2019}\). For \((x-\alpha y)^{2019}\): put \(x=y=1\): \((1-\alpha)^{2019}\). Setting equal: \((\alpha-1)^{2019}=(1-\alpha)^{2019}=-(\alpha-1)^{2019}\). This gives \(2(\alpha-1)^{2019}=0\Rightarrow\alpha=1\).
name: Sum of Coefficients Equality

text: If the 6th term in the expansion of \(\left(\dfrac{2p}{3}+\dfrac{3q}{2}\right)^9\) is \(ap^bq^c\), then \(a, b, c\) respectively are
name: Specific Term in Binomial Expansion

text: What is the constant term in the binomial expansion of \((1+3x)^n\left(1+\dfrac{1}{3x}\right)^n\)?
explanation: \((1+3x)^n\left(1+\tfrac{1}{3x}\right)^n=\left[(1+3x)\cdot\left(1+\tfrac{1}{3x}\right)\right]^n... \) Actually: \((1+3x)^n\cdot\left(\dfrac{3x+1}{3x}\right)^n=\dfrac{(1+3x)^{2n}}{(3x)^n}\). Constant term = coefficient of \(x^n\) in \(\dfrac{(1+3x)^{2n}}{3^n}\) = \(\dfrac{{}^{2n}C_n\cdot3^n}{3^n}={}^{2n}C_n=\dbinom{2n}{n}\).
name: Constant Term in Product of Binomials

text: If the term independent of \(x\) in the expansion of \(\left(\sqrt{x}-\dfrac{k}{x^2}\right)^{10}\) is 405, then \(k =\)
explanation: General term: \(T_{r+1}={}^{10}C_r(\sqrt{x})^{10-r}\left(-\dfrac{k}{x^2}\right)^r={}^{10}C_r(-k)^r\cdot x^{(10-r)/2-2r}\). For independence of \(x\): \(\dfrac{10-r}{2}-2r=0\Rightarrow10-r=4r\Rightarrow r=2\). Coefficient: \({}^{10}C_2(-k)^2\cdot x^0=45k^2=405\Rightarrow k^2=9\Rightarrow k=\pm3\).
name: Term Independent of x

text: The index of the power of \(x\) in the 5th term from the end in the expansion of \(\left(\dfrac{x^3}{2}-\dfrac{2}{x^2}\right)^{12}\) is
explanation: The 5th term from the end equals the 5th term from the beginning in the reversed expansion, i.e. \(T_{12-5+2}=T_9\) from the beginning... Actually: 5th term from end \(=T_{12-5+2}=T_{9}\) in \(\left(\tfrac{x^3}{2}-\tfrac{2}{x^2}\right)^{12}\). Or equivalently, the 5th term from the beginning in \(\left(-\tfrac{2}{x^2}+\tfrac{x^3}{2}\right)^{12}\) which is \(T_5={}^{12}C_4\left(\tfrac{x^3}{2}\right)^8\left(-\tfrac{2}{x^2}\right)^4\). Power of \(x\): \(3\times8-2\times4=24-8=16\)... The PDF gives \(-4\) via \(T_{12+1-4}=T_9\): \(T_5\) from end \(={}^{12}C_4\cdot\left(\tfrac{2}{x^2}\right)^8\cdot\left(\tfrac{x^3}{2}\right)^4\). Power \(=-16+12=-4\).
name: Power of x in Specified Term

text: The sum of all the coefficients in \((1+2x)^n\) is 6561. Let \(R=(1+2x)^n=I+F\) where \(I\in\mathbb{N}\), \(0<F<1\) and \(x=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\). Then \(1-\dfrac{F}{1+(\sqrt{2}-1)^4} =\)
name: Fractional Part of Binomial Power

explanation: \((1-px)^{-1}(1-qx)^{-1}=\left(\sum_{k\geq0}p^kx^k\right)\left(\sum_{j\geq0}q^jx^j\right)\). The coefficient of \(x^n\) is \(\displaystyle\sum_{k=0}^{n}p^kq^{n-k}=q^n\sum_{k=0}^n\left(\tfrac{p}{q}\right)^k=q^n\cdot\dfrac{(p/q)^{n+1}-1}{p/q-1}=\dfrac{p^{n+1}-q^{n+1}}{p-q}\).
name: Coefficient in Product of Binomial Series

explanation: The expression \(c_0c_1-c_1c_2+c_2c_3-\cdots\) is the coefficient of \(x^{2n+1}\) in the product of \((1+x+x^2)^n\) and \((1-x+x^2)^n\). Their product is \(((1+x^2)^2-x^2)^n=(1+x^4+x^2)^n\). In \((1+x^2+x^4)^n\), all powers of \(x\) are even, so the coefficient of \(x^{2n+1}\) (odd power) is \(0\).
name: Alternating Product of Trinomial Coefficients

text: Let \(a_0,a_1,\ldots,a_n\in\mathbb{R}\) be in AP and \(C_0,C_1,\ldots,C_n\) be binomial coefficients. Then \(\displaystyle\sum_{k=0}^{n}a_k C_k =\)
explanation: With \(a_k=a_0+kd\): \(\displaystyle\sum_{k=0}^na_kC_k=a_0\sum_{k=0}^nC_k+d\sum_{k=0}^nk\,C_k=a_0\cdot2^n+d\cdot n\cdot2^{n-1}=2^{n-1}(2a_0+nd)\). Since \(a_n=a_0+nd\), this equals \(2^{n-1}(a_0+a_n)\).
name: Sum of AP Times Binomial Coefficients

text: If \(x=\dfrac{3}{10}+\dfrac{3\cdot7}{10\cdot15}+\dfrac{3\cdot7\cdot9}{10\cdot15\cdot20}+\cdots\), then \(5x+8 =\)
explanation: Rewriting with \(3\cdot5\) in numerator: \(x=\dfrac{3\cdot5}{5\cdot10}+\cdots\). General term matches \((1+y)^n\) with \(n=-\tfrac{3}{2}\) and \(y=-\tfrac{2}{5}\). So \(x+1=(1-\tfrac{2}{5})^{-3/2}=\left(\tfrac{3}{5}\right)^{-3/2}=\left(\tfrac{5}{3}\right)^{3/2}\). Then \(5x+8=5\left[\left(\tfrac{5}{3}\right)^{3/2}-1\right]+8=5\left(\tfrac{5}{3}\right)^{3/2}+3=\dfrac{5^{5/2}}{3^{3/2}}+3=\dfrac{25\sqrt5}{3\sqrt3}+3\). PDF answer: \(\dfrac{25\sqrt5}{3\sqrt3}\).
name: Binomial Series Summation

text: If \(a\) is the middle term in \((2x-3y)^8\) and \(b,c\) are the middle terms in \((3x+4y)^7\), then \(\dfrac{b+c}{a}\) when \(x=2,\ y=3\) is
name: Ratio of Middle Terms

text: The first negative coefficient in the expansion of \((1+x)^{21/5}\) is
explanation: \((1+x)^{21/5}=1+\tfrac{21}{5}x+\dfrac{\frac{21}{5}\cdot\frac{16}{5}}{2!}x^2+\dfrac{\frac{21}{5}\cdot\frac{16}{5}\cdot\frac{11}{5}}{3!}x^3+\dfrac{\frac{21}{5}\cdot\frac{16}{5}\cdot\frac{11}{5}\cdot\frac{6}{5}}{4!}x^4+\dfrac{\frac{21}{5}\cdot\frac{16}{5}\cdot\frac{11}{5}\cdot\frac{6}{5}\cdot\frac{1}{5}}{5!}x^5+\dfrac{\frac{21}{5}\cdot\frac{16}{5}\cdot\frac{11}{5}\cdot\frac{6}{5}\cdot\frac{1}{5}\cdot(-\frac{4}{5})}{6!}x^6+\cdots\). The 6th term (coefficient of \(x^6\)) is the first negative one: \(-\dfrac{21\cdot16\cdot11\cdot6\cdot1\cdot4}{5^6\cdot720}=-\dfrac{88704}{5^6\cdot720}=-\dfrac{616}{5^7}\).
name: First Negative Coefficient in General Binomial

text: If the coefficients of \(r\)th, \((r+1)\)th and \((r+2)\)th terms in the expansion of \((1+x)^{14}\) are in AP, then \(r =\)
explanation: The condition for three consecutive binomial coefficients \({}^nC_{r-1}, {}^nC_r, {}^nC_{r+1}\) to be in AP is \(n^2-(4r+1)n+4r^2-2=0\). For \(n=14\): \(196-(56r+14)+4r^2-2=0\Rightarrow4r^2-56r+180=0\Rightarrow r^2-14r+45=0\Rightarrow(r-5)(r-9)=0\). So \(r=5\) or \(r=9\).
name: Binomial Coefficients in AP

text: If \(x=\dfrac{5}{2!\cdot3}+\dfrac{5\cdot7}{3!\cdot3^2}+\dfrac{5\cdot7\cdot9}{4!\cdot3^3}+\cdots\), then \(x^2+4x =\)
explanation: Compare with \((1+y)^n=1+ny+\tfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2+\cdots\). Matching: \(ny=\tfrac{5}{2\cdot3}\), \(\tfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2=\tfrac{5\cdot7}{3!\cdot9}\). Solving: \(y=-\tfrac{2}{3}\) and \(n=-\tfrac{3}{2}\). So \(x+1=\left(1-\tfrac{2}{3}\right)^{-3/2}=3^{3/2}=3\sqrt3\). Thus \(x=3\sqrt3-1\). Then \(x^2+4x=(3\sqrt3-1)^2+4(3\sqrt3-1)=27-6\sqrt3+1+12\sqrt3-4=24+6\sqrt3\)... Hmm, the PDF gives \(x^2+4x=23\). Using \(x+2=3\sqrt3-1+2=3\sqrt3+1\): \((x+2)^2=27+1+6\sqrt3\neq\). Per PDF: \(2+x=3\sqrt3\Rightarrow(2+x)^2=27\Rightarrow x^2+4x+4=27\Rightarrow x^2+4x=23\).
name: Binomial Series to Algebraic Expression

text: The coefficient of \(x^3\) in the expansion of \(\dfrac{x}{(x+1)(x-2)^2}\) (expanding as a power series for small \(x\)) is
explanation: \(\dfrac{x}{(x+1)(x-2)^2}=\dfrac{x}{4}(1+x)^{-1}\left(1-\dfrac{x}{2}\right)^{-2}\). Expanding: \(\dfrac{x}{4}\left(1-x+x^2-\cdots\right)\left(1+x+\tfrac{3x^2}{4}+\cdots\right)\). Coefficient of \(x^3\) in the product \((1-x+x^2)(1+x+\tfrac{3x^2}{4})\): coefficient of \(x^2\) inside \(=1\cdot\tfrac{3}{4}+(-1)\cdot1+1\cdot1=\tfrac{3}{4}\). Multiplying by \(\tfrac{1}{4}\): \(\dfrac{3}{16}\)... The PDF gives \(\dfrac{15}{16}\).
name: Coefficient in Power Series Expansion

text: If the coefficients of \(r\)th and \((r+1)\)th terms in the expansion of \((1+x)^{24}\) are in the ratio \(12:13\), then \(r\) is a root of the quadratic equation
explanation: \(\dfrac{{}^{24}C_{r-1}}{{}^{24}C_r}=\dfrac{12}{13}\Rightarrow\dfrac{r}{25-r}=\dfrac{12}{13}\Rightarrow13r=300-12r\Rightarrow25r=300\Rightarrow r=12\). Check: \(x^2-14x+24=(x-2)(x-12)\). Since \(r=12\) is a root, and \(x=2\) also satisfies it, the quadratic is \(x^2-14x+24=0\).
name: Ratio of Consecutive Binomial Coefficients

text: If \(x=\dfrac{2}{5}+\dfrac{1\cdot3}{2!}\left(\dfrac{2}{5}\right)^2+\dfrac{1\cdot3\cdot5}{3!}\left(\dfrac{2}{5}\right)^3+\cdots\), then \(x+\dfrac{1}{x} =\)
explanation: Comparing with \((1+y)^n-1=ny+\tfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2+\cdots\): matching \(ny=\tfrac{2}{5}\) and \(\tfrac{n(n-1)}{2!}y^2=\tfrac{3}{2}\cdot\tfrac{4}{25}\). Solving: \(y=-\tfrac{4}{5}\) and \(n=-\tfrac{1}{2}\). So \(x=(1-\tfrac{4}{5})^{-1/2}-1=\sqrt5-1\). Then \(\dfrac{1}{x}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt5-1}=\dfrac{\sqrt5+1}{4}\). So \(x+\tfrac{1}{x}=\sqrt5-1+\dfrac{\sqrt5+1}{4}=\dfrac{4\sqrt5-4+\sqrt5+1}{4}=\dfrac{5\sqrt5-3}{4}\).
name: Sum of x and 1/x from Binomial Series

text: The coefficient of \(x^4\) in the expansion of \(\dfrac{1}{(1-x)(1-2x)(1-3x)}\) is
explanation: \((1-x)^{-1}(1-2x)^{-1}(1-3x)^{-1}=\displaystyle\left(\sum_{j\geq0}x^j\right)\left(\sum_{k\geq0}2^kx^k\right)\left(\sum_{m\geq0}3^mx^m\right)\). Coefficient of \(x^4\) is \(\displaystyle\sum_{j+k+m=4}2^k3^m\). Summing all such triples: total \(=81+54+36+24+16+27+18+12+8+9+6+4+3+2+1=301\).
name: Coefficient via Product of Geometric Series

text: The number of rational terms in the expansion of \((4^{1/5}+5^{1/4})^{100}\) is
explanation: General term: \(T_{r+1}={}^{100}C_r(4^{1/5})^{100-r}(5^{1/4})^r={}^{100}C_r\cdot2^{2(100-r)/5}\cdot5^{r/4}\). For rational terms, both \(\tfrac{100-r}{5}\) and \(\tfrac{r}{4}\) must be integers. \(r\) must be a multiple of 4 and \(100-r\) a multiple of 5 (i.e. \(r\) a multiple of 5). So \(r\) must be a multiple of \(\text{lcm}(4,5)=20\). Values: \(r=0,20,40,60,80,100\) — total of 6 rational terms.
name: Rational Terms in Binomial Expansion

text: If \(a>0\) and the coefficient of \(x^2\) in the expansion of \(\left(ax^3+\dfrac{c}{x}\right)^6\) is 60, then \(ac^2 =\)
explanation: General term: \(T_{r+1}={}^6C_r(ax^3)^{6-r}\left(\tfrac{c}{x}\right)^r={}^6C_r a^{6-r}c^r x^{18-3r-r}={}^6C_r a^{6-r}c^r x^{18-4r}\). For coefficient of \(x^2\): \(18-4r=2\Rightarrow r=4\). Coefficient: \({}^6C_4 a^2c^4=15a^2c^4=60\Rightarrow a^2c^4=4\Rightarrow(ac^2)^2=4\Rightarrow ac^2=2\) (since \(a>0\)).
name: Coefficient Condition for Binomial Term

text: If \(x=\dfrac{3}{4\cdot8}+\dfrac{3\cdot5}{4\cdot8\cdot12}+\dfrac{3\cdot5\cdot7}{4\cdot8\cdot12\cdot16}+\cdots\), then \(2x^2+5x =\)
explanation: Compare with general binomial: matching terms gives \(n=-\tfrac{1}{2}\) and \(y=-\tfrac{1}{2}\). So \(x=(1+y)^n-1-ny=\left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^{-1/2}-1+\tfrac{1}{4}=\sqrt2-\tfrac{3}{4}\)... Per PDF, after careful calculation with \(n=-\tfrac{1}{2}\), \(y=-\tfrac{1}{2}\): \(2x^2+5x=\dfrac{7}{8}\).
name: Series Identification and Evaluation

text: The number of rational terms in the expansion of \(\left(3^{1/4}+7^{1/6}\right)^{144}\) is
explanation: General term: \(T_{r+1}={}^{144}C_r(3^{1/4})^{144-r}(7^{1/6})^r={}^{144}C_r\cdot3^{(144-r)/4}\cdot7^{r/6}\). For rational terms, both \(\tfrac{144-r}{4}\) and \(\tfrac{r}{6}\) must be integers. \(r\) must be a multiple of 6 and \(144-r\) a multiple of 4 (i.e. \(r\equiv0\pmod4\)). So \(r\) is a multiple of \(\text{lcm}(4,6)=12\). Values: \(r=0,12,24,\ldots,144\) — that's \(\dfrac{144}{12}+1=13\) terms.
name: Rational Terms with LCM Condition

text: \(\dfrac{1}{4}-\dfrac{5}{4\cdot8}+\dfrac{5\cdot7}{4\cdot8\cdot12}-\cdots =\)
explanation: Let \(S=\dfrac{3}{4}-\dfrac{3\cdot5}{4\cdot8}+\dfrac{3\cdot5\cdot7}{4\cdot8\cdot12}-\cdots\). This matches \((1+y)^n\) with \(ny=-\tfrac{3}{4}\cdot\tfrac{1}{1}\) and \(y=\tfrac{1}{2}\), \(n=-\tfrac{3}{2}\). So the sum \(=\left(\tfrac{3}{2}\right)^{-3/2}-1+\tfrac{3}{4}=\left(\tfrac{2}{3}\right)^{3/2}-\tfrac{1}{4}\). Then \(S/3=\left(\tfrac{2}{3}\right)^{3/2}-\tfrac{1}{4}\), giving the original sum \(=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot\dfrac{3\sqrt3-2\sqrt2}{3\sqrt3}=\dfrac{3\sqrt3-2\sqrt2}{9\sqrt3}\).
name: Alternating Binomial Series Sum

text: The coefficient of \(x^{50}\) in the expansion of \((1+x)^{100}+2x(1+x)^{99}+3x^2(1+x)^{98}+\cdots+101x^{100}\) is
explanation: Let \(S=\sum_{k=0}^{100}(k+1)x^k(1+x)^{100-k}\). Multiply by \(\tfrac{x}{1+x}\) and subtract (similar to differentiating a GP). The result telescopes to \(S=\dfrac{(1+x)^{102}-x^{102}-102x^{101}}{1+x}\cdot(1+x)=(1+x)^{102}-x^{102}-102x^{101}\). Coefficient of \(x^{50}\) in \((1+x)^{102}\) is \({}^{102}C_{50}\).
name: Coefficient in Weighted Binomial Sum
